title: China Tech Revolution Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: US officials alleged that SMIC, China's largest chipmaker, supplied chipmaking equipment and technical training to Iran's military — a potential sanctions violation that could trigger secondary sanctions against China's semiconductor expansion program. DeepSeek granted Huawei exclusive early access to its V4 AI model, deepening the China-domestic AI compute stack and bypassing US hardware restrictions; simultaneously, Tianjin Port achieved 88% automation using a Huawei-DeepSeek AI integration, a flagship showcase for Beijing's 15th Five-Year Plan 'AI+ action plan'. SEMICON China 2026 in Shanghai (March 25–27) projected China's domestic share of 22–40nm chip output to grow from 37% to 42% by 2028. ByteDance's valuation reached $550 billion following the finalized TikTok US divestiture deal in January 2026, in which Oracle, Silver Lake, and MGX each acquired 15% stakes.

title: China Tech Revolution Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan (2026–2030), passed at the National People's Congress on March 5, places AI and quantum computing at the heart of national strategy, mentioning AI over 50 times and pledging extraordinary measures to achieve global technological leadership. The plan's 'AI+ action plan' targets integration across every major economic sector, while funding commitments include at least 7% annual R&D growth. In a parallel development, China's cybersecurity regulator CNCERT issued a warning on March 10 about the viral OpenClaw AI agent, flagging data security risks even as major tech firms like Tencent continued to promote it publicly. On March 19, the US DOJ charged Super Micro co-founder Yih-Shyan Liaw and two associates with smuggling $2.5 billion in restricted Nvidia B200 and H200 AI chips to China through Southeast Asian intermediaries, marking the most significant escalation in US export control enforcement since the CHIPS Act era. The case drove Super Micro shares down more than 33% and underscored the growing enforcement gap that Beijing has been exploiting to acquire advanced AI hardware despite US sanctions.
